result.stdout:
Description:
  dSPACE COM tools (64Bit)

Usage:
  dscom [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  tlbexport <Assembly>                           Export the assembly to the specified type library
  tlbdump <TypeLibrary>                          Dump a type library
  tlbregister <TypeLibrary>                      Register a type library
  tlbunregister <TypeLibrary>                    Unregister a type library
  tlbembed <SourceTypeLibrary> <TargetAssembly>  Embeds a source type library into a target file
  regasm <TargetAssembly>                        Register an assembly

commands.payload:
Description:
  dSPACE COM tools (64Bit)

Usage:
  dscom [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  tlbexport <Assembly>                           Export the assembly to the specified type library
  tlbdump <TypeLibrary>                          Dump a type library
  tlbregister <TypeLibrary>                      Register a type library
  tlbunregister <TypeLibrary>                    Unregister a type library
  tlbembed <SourceTypeLibrary> <TargetAssembly>  Embeds a source type library into a target file
  regasm <TargetAssembly>                        Register an assembly

result.stdout:
Description:
  Register an assembly

Usage:
  dscom regasm <TargetAssembly> [options]

Arguments:
  <TargetAssembly>  File name of target assembly to receive the type library as a resource

Options:
  --asmpath <asmpath>                     Look for assembly references here
  --tlbrefpath, /tblrefpath <tlbrefpath>  Look for type library references here
  --tlb                                   Will create and register a typelibrary for the given assembly
  --codebase                              Will register the assembly with codebase
  --regfile <regfile>                     Will generate a registry entry text file (.reg) for the assembly instead of editing the registry directly
  --codebaseroot <codebaseroot>           Specify a relative path on a target machine that will contain both the COM host and target assembly. Only affects behavior of --regfile.
  --unregister                            Will unregister the assembly
  -?, -h, --help                          Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Register an assembly

Usage:
  dscom regasm <TargetAssembly> [options]

Arguments:
  <TargetAssembly>  File name of target assembly to receive the type library as a resource

Options:
  --asmpath <asmpath>                     Look for assembly references here
  --tlbrefpath, /tblrefpath <tlbrefpath>  Look for type library references here
  --tlb                                   Will create and register a typelibrary for the given assembly
  --codebase                              Will register the assembly with codebase
  --regfile <regfile>                     Will generate a registry entry text file (.reg) for the assembly instead of editing the registry directly
  --codebaseroot <codebaseroot>           Specify a relative path on a target machine that will contain both the COM host and target assembly. Only affects behavior of --regfile.
  --unregister                            Will unregister the assembly
  -?, -h, --help                          Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Dump a type library

Usage:
  dscom tlbdump <TypeLibrary> [options]

Arguments:
  <TypeLibrary>  File name of type library

Options:
  --out <out>                    File name of the output
  --tlbreference <tlbreference>  Type library used to resolve references
  --tlbrefpath <tlbrefpath>      Path used to resolve referenced type libraries
  --filterregex <filterregex>    Regex to filter the output
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Dump a type library

Usage:
  dscom tlbdump <TypeLibrary> [options]

Arguments:
  <TypeLibrary>  File name of type library

Options:
  --out <out>                    File name of the output
  --tlbreference <tlbreference>  Type library used to resolve references
  --tlbrefpath <tlbrefpath>      Path used to resolve referenced type libraries
  --filterregex <filterregex>    Regex to filter the output
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Embeds a source type library into a target file

Usage:
  dscom tlbembed <SourceTypeLibrary> <TargetAssembly> [options]

Arguments:
  <SourceTypeLibrary>  File name of type library
  <TargetAssembly>     File name of target assembly to receive the type library as a resource

Options:
  --index <index>  Index to use for resource ID for the type library. If omitted, defaults to 1. Must be a positive integer from 1 to 65535. [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Embeds a source type library into a target file

Usage:
  dscom tlbembed <SourceTypeLibrary> <TargetAssembly> [options]

Arguments:
  <SourceTypeLibrary>  File name of type library
  <TargetAssembly>     File name of target assembly to receive the type library as a resource

Options:
  --index <index>  Index to use for resource ID for the type library. If omitted, defaults to 1. Must be a positive integer from 1 to 65535. [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Export the assembly to the specified type library

Usage:
  dscom tlbexport <Assembly> [options]

Arguments:
  <Assembly>  File name of assembly to parse

Options:
  --out <out>                      File name of type library to be produced
  --tlbreference <tlbreference>    Type library used to resolve references
  --tlbrefpath <tlbrefpath>        Path used to resolve referenced type libraries
  --asmpath <asmpath>              Look for assembly references here
  --silent                         Suppresses all output except for errors
  --silence <silence>              Suppresses output for the given warning (Can not be used with /silent)
  --verbose                        Detailed log output
  --names <names>                  A file in which each line specifies the capitalization of a name in the type library.
  --overridename <overridename>    Overwrites the library name
  --overridetlbid <overridetlbid>  Overwrites the library id
  --createmissingdependenttlbs     Generate missing type libraries for referenced assemblies. (default true)
  --embed <embed>                  Embeds type library into the assembly. (default: false) [default: ]
  --index <index>                  If the switch --embed is specified, the index indicates the resource ID to be used for the embedded type library. Must be a number between 1 and 65535. Ignored if --embed not present. (default 1) [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Export the assembly to the specified type library

Usage:
  dscom tlbexport <Assembly> [options]

Arguments:
  <Assembly>  File name of assembly to parse

Options:
  --out <out>                      File name of type library to be produced
  --tlbreference <tlbreference>    Type library used to resolve references
  --tlbrefpath <tlbrefpath>        Path used to resolve referenced type libraries
  --asmpath <asmpath>              Look for assembly references here
  --silent                         Suppresses all output except for errors
  --silence <silence>              Suppresses output for the given warning (Can not be used with /silent)
  --verbose                        Detailed log output
  --names <names>                  A file in which each line specifies the capitalization of a name in the type library.
  --overridename <overridename>    Overwrites the library name
  --overridetlbid <overridetlbid>  Overwrites the library id
  --createmissingdependenttlbs     Generate missing type libraries for referenced assemblies. (default true)
  --embed <embed>                  Embeds type library into the assembly. (default: false) [default: ]
  --index <index>                  If the switch --embed is specified, the index indicates the resource ID to be used for the embedded type library. Must be a number between 1 and 65535. Ignored if --embed not present. (default 1) [default: 1]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Register a type library

Usage:
  dscom tlbregister <TypeLibrary> [options]

Arguments:
  <TypeLibrary>  File name of type library

Options:
  --foruser       Registered for use only by the calling user identity.
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
